tripOverview.intro: London offers the perfect blend of historic charm and modern romance for couples seeking adventure. From cozy pubs to world-class museums, the city provides endless opportunities for memorable moments together.
tripOverview.vibe: A whirlwind romantic adventure through London's most iconic neighborhoods, perfect for couples who love culture, food, and exploring together.

destinationOverview.bestTime: April is ideal with mild weather and blooming flowers in the parks.

attractions.name: Tower of London
attractions.desc: Historic castle home to the Crown Jewels

attractions.name: Westminster Abbey
attractions.desc: Gothic church where royal ceremonies take place

attractions.name: Tate Modern
attractions.desc: Contemporary art gallery in former power station

destinationOverview.transportation: London's extensive public transport system includes the Underground (Tube), buses, and river services that connect all major attractions efficiently. Black cabs are iconic but expensive, while Uber and Bolt offer convenient rideshare alternatives. An Oyster Card or contactless payment makes traveling on public transport seamless and cost-effective.
destinationOverview.tip: Download the Citymapper app for real-time transport updates and always stand right on escalators - Londoners take this seriously!
destinationOverview.safety: London is very safe for LGBTQ+ travelers with strong anti-discrimination laws and a vibrant queer community.
destinationOverview.airaloText: Stay connected with an Airalo eSIM for instant internet access to navigate London's streets and share your romantic moments.

days.intro: Start your London adventure exploring the city's most iconic historic landmarks.

morning.name: Tower of London
morning.desc: Explore the Crown Jewels and learn about London's royal history.

morning.name: Tower Bridge
morning.desc: Walk across the famous bridge and enjoy Thames views.

afternoon.name: Borough Market
afternoon.desc: Sample artisanal foods and browse the historic market stalls.

afternoon.name: Southbank Walk
afternoon.desc: Stroll along the Thames with views of the city skyline.

evening.name: The George Inn
evening.desc: Historic pub dinner with traditional British atmosphere.

evening.name: Thames Night Walk
evening.desc: Romantic evening walk with illuminated city views.

days.tip: Book Tower of London tickets online to skip queues and arrive early for smaller crowds.

days.intro: Dive into London's vibrant market scene and cultural treasures.

morning.name: British Museum
morning.desc: Explore world-famous artifacts including the Rosetta Stone.

morning.name: Russell Square
morning.desc: Peaceful garden square perfect for a romantic coffee break.

afternoon.name: Covent Garden Market
afternoon.desc: Browse unique shops and watch street performers.

afternoon.name: Neal's Yard
afternoon.desc: Colorful courtyard with charming cafes and shops.

evening.name: Dishoom Covent Garden
evening.desc: Bombay-style cafe with exceptional Indian vegetarian dishes.

evening.name: Royal Opera House
evening.desc: World-class performances in a stunning historic venue.

days.tip: The British Museum is huge - focus on specific galleries rather than trying to see everything.

days.intro: End your trip with artistic inspiration and romantic moments.

morning.name: Tate Modern
morning.desc: Contemporary art in a stunning converted power station.

morning.name: Millennium Bridge
morning.desc: Cross the Thames with views of St. Paul's Cathedral.

afternoon.name: St. Paul's Cathedral
afternoon.desc: Climb to the dome for panoramic city views.

afternoon.name: Hyde Park
afternoon.desc: Relaxing walk through London's most famous park.

evening.name: Sketch
evening.desc: Quirky pink restaurant with innovative vegetarian tasting menu.

evening.name: Heaven Nightclub
evening.desc: Iconic LGBTQ+ venue under the arches of Charing Cross.

days.tip: Book restaurant reservations in advance, especially for dinner - London's food scene is competitive.

food.intro: London's diverse culinary scene offers exceptional vegetarian and Indian cuisine alongside traditional British fare.
food.tip: Many pubs offer excellent vegetarian options and service charge is often included, so check your bill before tipping extra.

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden
neighborhoods.desc: Central location near theaters and restaurants

neighborhoods.name: Shoreditch
neighborhoods.desc: Trendy area with vibrant nightlife and markets
neighborhoods.whyStay: Hip restaurants and LGBTQ+ friendly venues

neighborhoods.name: South Bank
neighborhoods.whyStay: Near Tate Modern and Borough Market

luxury.name: The Savoy
luxury.vibe: Legendary luxury with Art Deco glamour

hotels.tip: Book accommodations early for April visits as it's peak tourist season with higher prices and limited availability.

getYourGuide.name: London Eye Fast Track
getYourGuide.desc: Skip-the-line access to the famous observation wheel with breathtaking city views.

getYourGuide.name: Westminster Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Guided tour covering Big Ben, Parliament, and royal landmarks with historical insights.

getYourGuide.name: Jack the Ripper Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Evening walking tour exploring Victorian London's most infamous mystery.

hiddenGems.name: Neal's Yard
hiddenGems.desc: Colorful courtyard tucked away in Covent Garden with unique shops and cafes.

hiddenGems.name: Little Venice
hiddenGems.desc: Charming canal area perfect for romantic walks and canal boat rides.

hiddenGems.name: God's Own Junkyard
hiddenGems.desc: Neon art gallery in a warehouse showcasing vintage signs and electric art.

packing.title: April London Packing
packing.intro: Pack layers for April's unpredictable weather with potential for both sun and showers.
packing.luggage: A carry-on suitcase should be sufficient for a 3-day trip with one personal item for essentials.
packing.tip: London weather changes quickly in April, so pack versatile pieces that can be layered for comfort throughout the day.

bookTrip.intro: Flights from Philadelphia to London typically cost $600-800 per person in April.
